facts.statement: HTTP status codes are organized into five families: 1xx informational, 2xx success, 3xx redirection, 4xx client error, and 5xx server error.
questions: How are HTTP status codes organized?
What are the five families of HTTP status codes?
What do the different ranges of HTTP status codes represent?
What do 1xx status codes mean?
What type of response does a 1xx status code represent?
What is the purpose of status codes in the 100 range?
What do 2xx status codes mean?
Which status code family indicates success?
What does a status code in the 200 range represent?
What do 3xx status codes mean?
Which status code range is used for redirects?
What type of response does a 3xx status code represent?
What do 4xx status codes mean?
Which status code family indicates client-side errors?
What does a status code in the 400 range represent?
What do 5xx status codes mean?
Which status code range indicates server-side errors?
What type of error does a 5xx status code represent?

facts.statement: Status code 200 means OK and indicates the request succeeded.
questions: What does HTTP status code 200 mean?
What status code indicates a successful request?
What does OK status mean in HTTP?

facts.statement: Status code 201 means Created and indicates a resource was created.
questions: What does HTTP status code 201 mean?
What status code indicates a resource was successfully created?
What is the difference between 200 and 201 status codes?

facts.statement: Status code 301 means Moved Permanently and indicates a permanent redirect.
questions: What does HTTP status code 301 mean?
What status code is used for permanent redirects?
How do you permanently redirect a URL in HTTP?

facts.statement: Status code 400 means Bad Request and indicates malformed request syntax.
questions: What does HTTP status code 400 mean?
What status code indicates a malformed request?
Why would I get a 400 Bad Request error?

facts.statement: Status code 401 means Unauthorized and indicates authentication is required.
questions: What does HTTP status code 401 mean?
What status code indicates authentication is needed?
What is the difference between 401 and 403 status codes?

facts.statement: Status code 403 means Forbidden and indicates the user is authenticated but not permitted to access the resource.
questions: What does HTTP status code 403 mean?
What status code indicates insufficient permissions?
Why would I get a 403 Forbidden error even though I'm logged in?

facts.statement: Status code 404 means Not Found and indicates the resource does not exist.
questions: What does HTTP status code 404 mean?
What status code indicates a missing resource?
Why am I getting a 404 error?

facts.statement: Status code 429 means Too Many Requests and indicates the client has been rate limited.
questions: What does HTTP status code 429 mean?
What status code indicates rate limiting?
Why am I getting a 429 Too Many Requests error?

facts.statement: Status code 500 means Internal Server Error and indicates a generic server failure.
questions: What does HTTP status code 500 mean?
What status code indicates a server error?
What causes a 500 Internal Server Error?

facts.statement: Status code 503 means Service Unavailable and indicates the server is temporarily overloaded or down.
questions: What does HTTP status code 503 mean?
What status code indicates the server is temporarily unavailable?
What is the difference between 500 and 503 status codes?
